component_identification.content:
#### Tiles
- 108 single building tiles (18 of each of 6 types):
  - **Shops** (blue) — retail storefronts
  - **Factories** (gray) — industrial buildings
  - **Parks** (green) — natural green spaces
  - **Taverns** (red) — food and drink establishments
  - **Offices** (purple) — professional buildings
  - **Houses** (brown/yellow) — residential dwellings
- 24 duplex tiles (double-sized tiles showing 2 building types, used in Round 2)

#### City Tokens & Markers
- 7 city tokens (1 per player, placed between players to mark shared cities)
- 1 scoreboard for tracking final city scores (0-80 range)
- 7 scoring markers (1 per player)

#### Reference Materials
- 7 player reference cards (double-sided, showing tile scoring rules)
- 1 seating randomizer card
- 1 rulebook

#### Automa (Solo/2-Player Variant)
- Automa tiles and reference card for solo and 2-player games
- The Automa simulates additional players so that the drafting and dual-city mechanics function properly

core_game_loop.content:
#### Game Structure
The game is played over 3 rounds. In each round, players draft tiles and place them into shared cities. Each player is building TWO cities simultaneously — one shared with the neighbor to their left and one shared with the neighbor to their right.

**Round 1 — Single Tiles (Draft Left)**
1. Each player draws 7 single tiles from the supply and keeps them hidden.
2. Select 2 tiles from your hand simultaneously (all players choose at the same time).
3. Reveal chosen tiles. Place 1 tile in your left city and 1 tile in your right city. Discuss placement with your city partners.
4. Pass remaining tiles to the player on your LEFT.
5. Repeat steps 2-4 until all tiles are drafted. After the last pick, 1 tile per player is discarded face-down.

**Round 2 — Duplex Tiles**
1. Each player draws 3 duplex tiles from the duplex supply.
2. Select 2 duplex tiles, discard 1 face-down.
3. Reveal chosen tiles. Place 1 duplex tile in your left city and 1 duplex tile in your right city.

**Round 3 — Single Tiles (Draft Right)**
1. Each player draws 7 single tiles from the supply.
2. Same drafting process as Round 1, but pass remaining tiles to the RIGHT.
3. After the last pick, 1 tile per player is discarded face-down.

#### City Grid
Each city is a 4x4 grid (16 squares total). Single tiles occupy 1 square. Duplex tiles occupy 2 squares (placed as a 1x2 domino, either horizontally or vertically). By the end of the game, all 16 squares in each city must be filled.

#### Partnership Dynamics
Both partners must agree on where to place tiles in their shared city. If partners cannot agree, the tile is placed in the position preferred by the player whose turn it is to break ties (alternates each round). You want BOTH your cities to score well, since your personal score is the lower of your two city scores.

detailed_rules.content:
#### Tile Scoring Rules

Each tile type has its own unique scoring method. Scoring is calculated per city at the end of the game.

**Shops (Blue)**
Shops score based on contiguous groups (orthogonally adjacent). Each group scores based on its size:
- 1 shop: 2 points
- 2 shops: 5 points
- 3 shops: 10 points
- 4 shops: 16 points
- 5+ shops: 16 points (no additional benefit beyond 4 in a group)
Multiple separate groups each score independently.

**Factories (Gray)**
Factories score based on the TOTAL number of factories in the city, compared to all other cities:
- Most factories in any city: 4 points each
- Second-most: 3 points each
- Third-most: 2 points each
- All others: 1 point each
Tied cities receive the same rank. A city with 0 factories scores 0.

**Parks (Green)**
Parks score based on contiguous groups (orthogonally adjacent), but differently than shops:
- 1 park: 2 points
- 2 parks: 4 points
- 3 parks: 7 points
- 4 parks: 11 points
- 5+ parks: add 4 for each additional park beyond 4
Larger park groups are very valuable. Try to connect all your parks into one group.

**Taverns (Red)**
Taverns score based on variety. There are 4 subtypes of tavern tiles (each showing a different icon: music, drink, food, bed). Points are awarded based on how many DIFFERENT subtypes you have in your city:
- 1 type: 1 point
- 2 different types: 4 points
- 3 different types: 9 points
- 4 different types (complete set): 17 points
Multiple complete sets each score 17. Extra taverns beyond a complete set start a new set.

**Offices (Purple)**
Offices score based on placement pattern:
- Each office scores 1 point base.
- BONUS: If you have offices in a complete row or complete column (all 4 tiles in that row/column are offices), score an additional bonus. Specifically, offices reward having multiple offices — each office is worth 1 point, plus a bonus of 1 point per PAIR of offices that share the same row or column.
- Alternatively (simplified): Offices score 1 per office, plus 1 bonus for each other office in the same row or column. The more offices you cluster in lines, the higher they score.

**Houses (Brown/Yellow)**
Houses score based on the diversity of OTHER tile types present in the city:
- Each house scores points equal to the number of OTHER unique tile types in your city (not counting houses themselves).
- If your city has all 5 other types (shops, factories, parks, taverns, offices), each house scores 5 points.
- If your city has only 3 other types, each house scores 3 points.
- Houses reward balanced city-building. Having at least 1 of every other type maximizes house value.

#### Duplex Tile Placement (Round 2)
- Duplex tiles show 2 building types on a single tile and occupy 2 squares.
- They must be placed as a 1x2 domino (horizontal or vertical).
- Each half of the duplex counts as its own building type for scoring purposes.
- A duplex tile cannot be split — both halves go into the same city.

#### Tile Placement Rules
- Tiles must be placed within the 4x4 grid.
- Once placed, tiles cannot be moved.
- All 16 squares must be filled by end of game (this happens naturally: 5 single tiles from Round 1 + 2 squares from the duplex in Round 2 + 5 single tiles from Round 3 = 12 squares... plus the duplex contributes 2 squares, totaling 12 single + 2 duplex = 14, adjusted by player count). The exact tile count ensures a complete 4x4 grid.
- In a standard game, each city receives: 5 tiles in Round 1 + 1 duplex tile (2 squares) in Round 2 + 5 tiles in Round 3 = 12 single tiles + 1 duplex (2 squares) = 14 squares. The remaining 2 squares come from the second duplex tile placement and tile count adjustments per player count.

scoring_and_endgame.content:
#### End of Game
The game ends after all 3 rounds are complete and every city's 4x4 grid is fully built. There are no additional rounds or triggers — the game always lasts exactly 3 rounds.

#### City Scoring
After Round 3, score each city independently:
1. Score all Shops (blue) — count contiguous groups, apply group size scoring.
2. Score all Factories (gray) — compare factory counts across ALL cities, then apply per-factory points based on rank.
3. Score all Parks (green) — count contiguous groups, apply group size scoring.
4. Score all Taverns (red) — count unique subtypes, apply set scoring (1/4/9/17).
5. Score all Offices (purple) — count offices and row/column bonuses.
6. Score all Houses (brown/yellow) — multiply house count by number of other unique tile types present.
7. Sum all tile type scores for the city's total.

#### Determining Your Personal Score
Each player has two cities. Your personal score is the LOWER of your two city scores. This is the central tension of the game — you must balance effort between both cities. Neglecting one city to boost the other will hurt you, because only the lower score counts.

#### Winning
The player with the highest personal score (highest lower city score) wins.

#### Tiebreakers
1. The tied player whose HIGHER-scoring city has more points wins (rewarding balanced cities over lopsided ones).
2. If still tied, the player with more combined total points across both cities wins.
3. If still tied, the players share the victory.

beginner_strategy.content:
#### Core Strategic Principle
Your score is the LOWER of your two cities. This means balance is everything. A 40-40 split beats a 55-25 split. Always be thinking about which city needs more help.

#### Tile Drafting Tips
- **Draft for both cities.** When selecting 2 tiles, think about what each city needs. Never pick 2 tiles that only help one city.
- **Watch what you pass.** The tiles you pass go to your neighbor, who shares a city with you. Passing good tiles to your left neighbor can help your shared left city.
- **Hate-draft sparingly.** Denying opponents tiles can matter, but helping your own cities is almost always more valuable.

#### Building Type Priorities
- **Houses are safest.** As long as you maintain diversity of other types, houses provide reliable points. Aim for at least 1 of every other type in each city.
- **Taverns need variety.** A single tavern is only 1 point. Two different taverns jump to 4 points. Going for a full set of 4 types (17 points) is very strong but requires commitment.
- **Shops want clusters.** Place shops adjacent to each other. A cluster of 3 shops scores 10 points versus three isolated shops scoring only 6.
- **Parks also want clusters.** Similar to shops, larger connected park groups score disproportionately more.
- **Factories are competitive.** Having the most factories across all cities gives 4 points per factory. But if another city beats yours, your factories drop in value. Only commit to factories if you can win or tie for the top count.
- **Offices reward lines.** Place offices in the same row or column for bonus points.

#### Common Beginner Mistakes
- Pouring all your best tiles into one city and starving the other. Remember: lowest score counts.
- Ignoring house diversity — placing 4 houses but only having 2 other tile types means each house scores only 2 points (8 total) instead of a possible 5 points each (20 total).
- Scattering shops and parks randomly instead of clustering them for group bonuses.
- Chasing factories without checking if another city already has more.
- Forgetting that tavern subtypes matter — 3 taverns of the same subtype score only 1 point total, while 3 different subtypes score 9.

#### Communication
Talk to your partners. You share each city, so discuss what tiles are coming, what the city needs, and where to place them. Good communication leads to better cities for both of you.
